evals.prompt: Write Vitest unit tests for Next.js components
evals.expected_output: import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react'; it('shows title', () => { render(<PostCard title='Hello'/>); expect(screen.getByText('Hello')).toBeInTheDocument(); }); configure vitest.config.ts with react plugin

evals.prompt: Set up Playwright E2E tests for a Next.js app
evals.expected_output: npx playwright install; test('login flow', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('/login'); await page.fill('[name=email]', 'user@test.com'); await page.click('button[type=submit]'); await expect(page).toHaveURL('/dashboard'); })

evals.prompt: Mock API calls with MSW in Next.js tests
evals.expected_output: const server = setupServer(http.get('/api/posts', () => HttpResponse.json([{id:1,title:'Test'}]))); beforeAll(() => server.listen()); afterEach(() => server.resetHandlers()); afterAll(() => server.close())

evals.prompt: Test a Server Component in Next.js
evals.expected_output: Use Playwright E2E to test full RSC output end-to-end; for unit testing data layer, test the async function directly (getUser() etc.); RTL cannot render async Server Components — use E2E for RSC behavior

evals.prompt: Configure test database isolation for Next.js
evals.expected_output: Use separate .env.test with TEST_DATABASE_URL; run migrations in globalSetup; wrap each test in transaction + rollback; or use Testcontainers for fully isolated DB per test suite
